description: Kenya 16 standard pavement structure types from RDM 3.4. Each structure was derived using mechanistic-empirical analysis (multi-layer elastic theory) and verified for its designated traffic and subgrade class range.

material_codes.GCS: Graded Crushed Stone -- high-quality crushed aggregate for base/subbase, per RDM 3.3 Chapter 8
material_codes.HSM: Hydraulically Stabilised Material -- cement or lime treated material, per RDM 3.3
material_codes.HPS: Hand-Packed Stone -- labour-intensive base construction method
material_codes.HIG: Hydraulically Improved Gravel -- improved natural gravel with hydraulic binder
material_codes.G45: Natural gravel with minimum soaked CBR >= 45%
material_codes.G30: Natural gravel with minimum soaked CBR >= 30%
material_codes.G15: Selected fill / capping with minimum soaked CBR >= 15%
material_codes.AC_base: Asphalt concrete base layer (higher binder content than wearing course)
material_codes.surface_dressing: Single or double surface dressing per Standard Specification

structures.name: Granular base, granular subbase
structures.description: Standard granular structure for light to medium traffic on good subgrade
structures.notes: Most economical structure where GCS is available. Surface dressing thickness not fixed.

structures.name: Granular base, granular subbase (heavy duty)
structures.description: Enhanced granular structure for medium to heavy traffic
structures.notes: AC surfacing provides better waterproofing than surface dressing for higher traffic.

structures.name: Granular base, cemented subbase
structures.description: Granular base with stabilised subbase for weaker subgrades
structures.notes: HSM subbase provides structural improvement for moderate subgrade.

structures.name: Granular base, cemented subbase (heavy duty)
structures.description: Enhanced structure with cemented subbase for heavy traffic
structures.notes: Suitable for KeNHA Class B roads with medium-heavy traffic.

structures.name: Cemented base, cemented subbase
structures.description: Fully cemented structure for weaker subgrades
structures.notes: Provides structural capacity on weak subgrades. Risk of shrinkage cracking -- design for crack control.

structures.name: Cemented base, cemented subbase (heavy duty)
structures.description: Heavy-duty cemented structure for high traffic on moderate subgrade
structures.notes: AC surfacing essential for heavy traffic to waterproof cemented layers.

structures.name: Bituminous base, granular subbase
structures.description: Bituminous base for heavy traffic with granular subbase
structures.notes: Requires asphalt plant capability. Common for KeNHA trunk roads.

structures.name: Bituminous base, granular subbase (heavy duty)
structures.description: Heavy-duty bituminous base for very heavy to maximum traffic
structures.notes: For major national highways and expressways. Requires good quality GCS subbase.

structures.name: Bituminous base, cemented subbase
structures.description: Bituminous base with cemented subbase for moisture-susceptible areas
structures.notes: Better moisture protection than ST07. Preferred in wet climate zones.

structures.name: Bituminous base, cemented subbase (heavy duty)
structures.description: Heavy-duty bituminous with cemented subbase for maximum traffic
structures.notes: For expressways and major corridors. Combines structural capacity with moisture protection.

structures.name: Hand-packed stone base
structures.description: Labour-intensive base construction for low-volume rural roads
structures.notes: Kenya-specific innovation for KeRRA rural roads. Uses local labour and stone.

structures.name: Hydraulically improved gravel base
structures.description: Stabilised natural gravel base for rural roads
structures.notes: Improves marginal gravel quality to base standard using small cement additions.

structures.name: Full-depth bituminous
structures.description: All-bituminous pavement for maximum traffic on good subgrade
structures.notes: Highest cost but best fatigue performance. For expressways and critical corridors.

structures.name: Inverted structure (bituminous over cemented)
structures.description: Inverted pavement with bituminous layers over thick cemented layer
structures.notes: The thick cemented subbase provides stiffness while bituminous layers resist fatigue cracking.

structures.name: Composite with capping
structures.description: Structure for weak subgrades requiring capping layer
structures.notes: Capping layer required for weak subgrades (S1-S3). G15 capping raises effective foundation strength.

structures.name: Low-volume sealed
structures.description: Minimal sealed structure for very light traffic
structures.notes: Most basic sealed structure. Natural gravel base only, suitable for very low traffic.

notes: All 16 standard structures derived from mechanistic-empirical analysis (MLET + transfer functions)
Structure applicability ranges are indicative -- verify against RDM 3.4 Section 4.5 details
Material specifications per RDM 3.3 Chapter 8 and Standard Specification
Layer thicknesses shown are typical -- RDM 3.4 may specify ranges for some structures
Surfacing thickness null indicates surface dressing (no fixed layer thickness)
Capping null indicates capping layer not required for that structure
Kenya material codes are NOT equivalent to Uganda codes -- do not cross-reference
